title: The 1935 Pole That Charged Cars Rent
desc:
July 16, 1935: Oklahoma City bolted the world's first parking meters to the curb — about 175 machines across 14 downtown blocks. A nickel bought an hour; overstaying cost a $20 fine — 400 hours of nickels. A month in, the first recorded ticket went to a preacher who'd dashed inside to get change FOR the meter. Case dismissed. The machines won anyway: Dallas estimated $120,000 a year from meters by 1936, repaying installation in about six months, and 140,000+ meters were collecting across America by the early 1940s. Dreamed up by newspaperman Carl Magee, engineered by two Oklahoma State professors. Machines that earn while you sleep — a 90-year-old playbook.

#BrokeToBuilt #history #invention
